summary.intent: Implement the agnostic core for a ScrollArea component (task #279), following the project's Named Effect intents pattern where adapters own timers and the core emits Effect intents.
summary.outcome: ScrollArea core fully implemented with 43 unit tests, 13 snapshot fixtures, conformance and proptest harnesses, 97.48% line coverage, and all 6 Codex review issues resolved. PR #699 pushed to GitHub.

repo: Named Effect intents pattern: agnostic core never calls set_timeout directly; adapters own timers and the core emits Effect intents (e.g., Effect::AutoHide)
context_changed is set whenever an apply closure runs, not on value diff — closures must be carefully scoped
context_only closures require explicit &mut Context type annotation
set_state_for_test is #[cfg(test)] in ars-core and is unavailable cross-crate
Delay fields use plain Duration (not Option<Duration>) with a named DEFAULT_* constant, matching tooltip open_delay/close_delay convention
CI uses a floating @stable toolchain (currently 1.96.0), which can expose pre-existing clippy regressions in unrelated code
on_props_changed must be implemented to avoid stale prop sync bugs when props change after initial mount
scrollbar_cross_size belongs in Props for adapter spec parity, not as dead internal state

code.finding: Scrollbar visibility should be gated by orientation (allows_x/allows_y on ScrollOrientation) — initially omitted as an intentional audit decision, reversed after Codex review
on_props_changed was not implemented, causing stale prop sync when props update after mount — SyncProps event + on_props_changed added to fix
ThumbDragMove clamp was missing the upper bound [0, track_size - thumb_size], allowing scroll position to exceed max when dragging past track end
Resize event did not clear stale visibility flags in Hover/Scroll modes — overflow removal would leave scrollbar incorrectly visible
ThumbDragEnd never re-emitted AutoHide effect when ending drag in Scroll mode, leaving scrollbar permanently visible after drag
Hover state became stuck visible after root-leave while over the scrollbar track — required tracking hovering_root separately in Context
Pre-existing clippy::unnecessary_option_map_or_else error exposed by Rust 1.96.0 stable — map_or_else(.., identity) → unwrap_or_else(..) blocks any clippy run on ars-components

workflow: Spec examples can embed platform-specific calls (e.g., set_timeout) that must be converted to Effect intents before implementation
Audit new implementations in 3 phases: spec/impl drift, adjacent surfaces (adapter specs, manifests, catalog), and coverage before submitting
Post-review: resolve all Codex P2 threads, update proptest invariants and spec in the same commit to keep them in sync
snapshot-reviewed label required on PRs that add .snap fixture files
cargo xci-fast is the pre-push gate — run before committing

friction: Spec example used platform set_timeout directly instead of the Named Effect intents pattern — required manual conversion before implementation
Axis enum was undefined in the spec and had to be defined locally
context_only closures required multiple rounds of adding explicit &mut Context annotations before the build passed
insta --accept blocked by pre-existing i18n-gated highlight snapshots not referenced without the i18n feature — required manual snapshot promotion
Background snapshot regeneration jobs raced, producing stale pending files that needed manual cleanup
Pre-existing pagination clippy error in ars-components blocked all clippy runs on the crate, requiring an incidental fix
Codex review latency required cancelling the background poller; review was not available until the following user prompt
open_items: PR #699 is open but Codex has not issued a final 👍 verdict — merge and issue close (#279) pending Codex approval + green CI
Leptos and Dioxus adapter implementations for ScrollArea are out of scope for this task and not yet done
Foundation promotion deferred: local Axis{X,Y} diverges from ars-dom's Axis{Horizontal,Vertical} — not resolved, noted as a cross-cutting refactor
Codex review threads replied to but thread resolution not yet confirmed — may require a follow-up pass
